description: Mutating a container (push_back/erase) while iterating it, invalidating the iterator, pointer, or reference in use.
body: Failure mode: a loop holds an iterator or reference into a std::vector/map and then inserts or erases, reallocating or removing the node so the iterator dangles and the next dereference is UB. Detection signals: ASan heap-use-after-free inside the loop; crashes only past a capacity threshold (vector reallocation); corrupted values after erase. Do not modify a container while iterating with a stale iterator. Safer fallback: use the return value of erase (it = v.erase(it)), reserve capacity before bulk insert, or collect changes and apply after the loop, then verify with ASan across a growth boundary.
